title: Implement HAL core service stubs and capability discovery
description: Why this exists: application and protocol code need one place to ask what a board can do and to call optional services without scattered board-specific conditionals. What needs to be done: implement core HAL service registry/capability discovery for Zephyr firmware with no-op/stub providers for absent features. Include status APIs for radio presence, display availability, input availability, LED availability, local-client transports, storage, battery/PMIC, location provider availability, date/time provider availability, and board identity. Acceptance criteria: public API returns deterministic unavailable/unsupported errors for absent services; headless/native test configuration exercises all stubs; app/UI code can depend on HAL capability calls instead of board ifdefs; no dynamic allocation in hot paths; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Implementation evidence (2026-07-01):
- Added HAL capability status APIs in lichen/subsys/lichen/hal/include/lichen/hal.h and lichen/subsys/lichen/hal/hal.c: lichen_hal_capability_status(), named status wrappers for LoRa/BLE-local/serial-local/GNSS/battery/PMIC/buttons/LEDs/display/external flash, plus location/time provider status.
- Added single-capability validation so zero, multi-bit masks, and unknown bits return -EINVAL; absent capabilities return -ENOTSUP; configured-but-not-ready devices flow through getters as -ENODEV.
- Added zephyr,slip-uart serial-local support in the HAL BUILD_ASSERT and serial getter for SLIP bridge boards.
- Added LICHEN_HAL_GNSS_DEVICE compile-time macro so GNSS callback registration can avoid app-level devicetree expressions.
- Migrated gateway and puck app LoRa/GNSS runtime access to HAL getters and HAL identity. meshtastic_adapter now uses HAL identity/caps rather than direct CONFIG_BOARD fallback.
- Added/adjusted board capability declarations for gateway and puck board configs. Removed T-Beam Supreme LoRa/HAL capability overclaim because its overlay is still a placeholder for a missing board definition.
- Added HAL tests for capability status, invalid query rejection, deterministic headless status wrappers, and qemu_x86 integration platform coverage.
- Created follow-up project-LICHEN-xcxf for remaining lower-level native UART direct devicetree access in lichen/lib/native/native.c.

Review evidence:
- Explorer agents Gauss/Tesla mapped app call sites and acceptance gaps; implemented app migrations and created project-LICHEN-xcxf for the remaining native transport dependency pass.
- First review round: Avicenna found P2 T-Echo puck BLE-local overclaim; fixed by removing BLE-local capability from puck t_echo config. Dirac found P2 GNSS callback still using direct devicetree and qemu_x86 missing from integration_platforms; fixed both.
- Second review round after fixes: Aristotle found P2 T-Beam Supreme LoRa/HAL overclaim; fixed by removing the claim from puck tbeam_supreme.conf. Poincare reported no P0/P1/P2.
- Final post-fix reviews: Lovelace and Ptolemy both reported no P0/P1/P2 findings. Non-blocking gaps: no dedicated positive unit test for serial-local zephyr,slip-uart; board builds enforce the HAL/devicetree contract. Lower-level native UART direct devicetree access is tracked by project-LICHEN-xcxf.

- Local qemu_x86 HAL run: west build -d build/hal_qemu_h7t54 -t run: PASS by PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL; hal suite 5 pass, 0 fail, 1 skip. QEMU wrapper required manual cleanup after success.
- Local qemu_x86 gateway build: west build -p always -b qemu_x86 lichen/apps/gateway -d build/gateway_qemu_h7t54: PASS. RAM 235676 B / 31 MB. Warnings: CONFIG_TIMER_RANDOM_GENERATOR testing-only; Zephyr no-source libraries drivers__entropy and drivers__spi.
- Local native_sim attempt failed as expected on macOS because Zephyr POSIX architecture is Linux-only.
- EC2 Linux native_sim loopback HAL validation on EBS clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t54: PASS using west build -p always -b native_sim /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t54/lichen/tests/hal -d /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t54/build/hal_native_loopback_h7t54 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t54/lichen -DCONF_FILE="prj.conf;prj_lora_loopback.conf" -DDTC_OVERLAY_FILE=boards/native_sim_lora_loopback.overlay, then zephyr.exe. Result PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL; 5 pass, 0 fail, 1 skip. Warning: no sources for loramac-node. Used CCACHE_DIR=/tmp/lichen-ccache-h7t54 due EBS ccache permissions.
- Dynamic allocation scan/reviews found no new malloc/calloc/realloc/free/k_malloc/k_calloc/k_free use in reviewed HAL/app/test paths.

close_reason: Completed no-hardware HAL capability/status stubs, app migrations, tests, reviews, EC2 native_sim validation, cleanup, and follow-up project-LICHEN-xcxf for native UART HAL migration.
